title: rosa communis subvar. fastigiata
intro: Rosa communis subvar. fastigiata, also known as the wild rose or dog rose, is a shrub native to Europe and Western Asia, characterized by its fragrant pink to white flowers and red hips. The plant has been used in herbal medicine for centuries, with its medicinal properties attributed to its high content of flavonoids, phenolic acids, and essential oils, which exhibit anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Rosa communis subvar. fastigiata include reducing inflammation and pain, improving digestion, and boosting the immune system, making it a popular remedy for various ailments such as arthritis, digestive issues, and colds. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions, as well as used in topical creams and salves, to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
